overview.title: DazScript Environment Overview
overview.content:
DazScript is a JavaScript-based scripting environment for DAZ Studio.

**Global Objects:**
- `Scene` (DzScene) - The current scene
- `App` (DzApp) - The application object
- `MainWindow` - Main window reference

**Script Format:**
Scripts must be wrapped in an IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression) if they return a value:
```javascript
(function(){
    return Scene.getNumNodes();
})();
```

**Error Handling:**
- `throw new Error("msg")` - Propagates as error with line number
- `throw "string"` - Propagates as plain error message

**Accessing Arguments:**
Use the `args` variable to access passed arguments:
```javascript
var myValue = args.myKey;
var name = args.characterName;
```

scene.title: Scene Management
scene.content:
## Creating a New Scene

**Clear the current scene:**
```javascript
Scene.clear();
```

## Saving a Scene

**Save to file:**
```javascript
var success = Scene.saveToFile("/path/to/scene.duf");
if (!success) throw new Error("Failed to save scene");
```

**Get current scene file path:**
```javascript
var filePath = Scene.getFilename(); // Empty string if unsaved
```

## Loading Content

**Load/merge content file:**
```javascript
var contentMgr = App.getContentMgr();
contentMgr.openFile("/path/to/character.duf", true); // true = merge
contentMgr.openFile("/path/to/scene.duf", false); // false = replace
```

## Selection

**Get primary selection:**
```javascript
var selected = Scene.getPrimarySelection();
if (selected) {
    print("Selected: " + selected.getLabel());
}
```

**Set selection:**
```javascript
var node = Scene.findNodeByLabel("Genesis 9");
Scene.setPrimarySelection(node);
```

## Node Management

**Get total node count:**
```javascript
var count = Scene.getNumNodes(); // Can be 3000+ in complex scenes
```

**Remove a node:**
```javascript
var node = Scene.findNodeByLabel("Unwanted Object");
if (node) {
    Scene.removeNode(node);
}
```

properties.title: Node Properties and Transforms

content.title: Browsing and Loading Content

coordinates.title: Coordinate System and Positioning
coordinates.content:
## DAZ Studio Coordinate System

**Axes:**
- **X** - Left/Right (positive = right, negative = left)
- **Y** - Up/Down (positive = up, negative = down)
- **Z** - Forward/Back (positive = forward, negative = back)

**Units:** Centimeters (cm)

## Genesis Figure Orientation

**Genesis 8 and 9 figures face +Z (forward)**

**Camera positioning:**
- Place camera at **positive Z** to see the face (front view)
- Place camera at **negative Z** for back view
- Place camera at **positive X** for right side view
- Place camera at **negative X** for left side view

## Common Height References

**Genesis 9 character (approximately 180cm tall):**
- Feet: Y = 0
- Waist: Y = 100
- Chest: Y = 135
- Head: Y = 163
- Top of head: Y = 175

## Positioning Examples

**Front portrait camera:**
```javascript
camera.findProperty("XTranslate").setValue(0);   // Centered
camera.findProperty("YTranslate").setValue(160); // Eye level
camera.findProperty("ZTranslate").setValue(150); // In front
camera.aimAt(new DzVec3(0, 160, 0));
```

**Side view camera:**
```javascript
camera.findProperty("XTranslate").setValue(150); // Right side
camera.findProperty("YTranslate").setValue(140); // Chest level
camera.findProperty("ZTranslate").setValue(0);   // Side on
camera.aimAt(new DzVec3(0, 140, 0));
```

**Key light (front-right, elevated):**
```javascript
light.findProperty("XTranslate").setValue(150);  // Right
light.findProperty("YTranslate").setValue(200);  // Above
light.findProperty("ZTranslate").setValue(150);  // In front
light.aimAt(new DzVec3(0, 140, 0)); // Aim at chest
```

hierarchy.title: Scene Hierarchy and Node Relationships
hierarchy.content:
## Understanding Scene Hierarchy

**DAZ Studio uses a parent-child hierarchy** for organizing scene nodes:
- **Root nodes** - Top-level nodes with no parent (figures, props, cameras, lights)
- **Child nodes** - Nodes parented to other nodes (bones are children of figures)
- **Descendants** - All nodes below a node in the hierarchy (children, grandchildren, etc.)

**Common hierarchies:**
- **Figure skeleton** - Figure root → hip → pelvis → thighBend → shin → foot
- **Props** - Clothing items parented to figure bones
- **Scene organization** - Groups of objects parented together

## Node Relationship APIs

### Getting Parent

**DazScript method:**
```javascript
var node = Scene.findNodeByLabel("lHand");
var parent = node.getNodeParent();

if (parent) {
    print("Parent: " + parent.getLabel());
} else {
    print("Node is a root (no parent)");
}
```

**MCP tool:**
```python
result = daz_get_parent("lHand")
if result["parent"]:
    print(f"Parent: {result['parent']['label']}")
else:
    print("Node is a root")
```

### Listing Children

**DazScript method:**
```javascript
var node = Scene.findNodeByLabel("Genesis 9");
var numChildren = node.getNumNodeChildren();

for (var i = 0; i < numChildren; i++) {
    var child = node.getNodeChild(i);
    print("Child " + i + ": " + child.getLabel());
}
```

**MCP tool:**
```python
result = daz_list_children("Genesis 9")
for child in result["children"]:
    print(f"Child: {child['label']} ({child['type']})")
```

## Parenting Operations

### Setting Parent

**CRITICAL: `setNodeParent()` requires a boolean parameter**

```javascript
var child = Scene.findNodeByLabel("Sword");
var parent = Scene.findNodeByLabel("rHand");

// maintainWorldTransform: true = keep world position, false = keep local transform
child.setNodeParent(parent, true);

print("Sword parented to hand");
```

**Parameter meanings:**
- `true` - Maintain world-space position (adjust local transform)
- `false` - Keep local transform values (node will move in world space)

**MCP tool usage:**
```python
# Attach sword to right hand
daz_set_parent("Sword", "rHand", maintain_world_transform=True)

# Camera follows figure
daz_set_parent("Camera 1", "Genesis 9", maintain_world_transform=True)
```

## Using MCP Hierarchy Tools

### daz_get_node_hierarchy
```python
# Get skeleton hierarchy with depth limit
hierarchy = daz_get_node_hierarchy("Genesis 9", max_depth=4)
print(f"Total descendants: {hierarchy['totalDescendants']}")

# Print hierarchy tree
def print_tree(node, indent=0):
    print("  " * indent + f"{node['label']} ({node['type']})")
    for child in node.get('children', []):
        print_tree(child, indent + 1)

print_tree(hierarchy["hierarchy"])
```

### daz_list_children
```python
# List bones directly under hip
result = daz_list_children("hip")
for child in result["children"]:
    print(f"  {child['label']}")
# Output: pelvis, lThighBend, rThighBend, abdomenLower
```

### daz_get_parent
```python
# Find what bone contains a specific bone
result = daz_get_parent("lHand")
print(f"Hand is child of: {result['parent']['label']}")
# Output: Hand is child of: lForearmBend
```

### daz_set_parent
```python
# Attach weapon to hand
daz_set_parent("Sword", "rHand", maintain_world_transform=True)

# Camera follows character
daz_set_parent("Camera 1", "head", maintain_world_transform=True)
```

## Key Takeaways for Hierarchy

1. **Parent-child relationships** - Nodes form a tree structure
2. **setNodeParent() boolean parameter** - true maintains world position, false keeps local values
3. **World vs local transforms** - Parenting changes local values to maintain world position
4. **MCP tools available** - Use `daz_get_node_hierarchy`, `daz_list_children`, `daz_get_parent`, `daz_set_parent`
5. **Genesis skeleton is deep** - ~100+ bones, use depth limits when traversing
6. **Common use cases** - Attach props, parent cameras, organize scenes

batch.title: Batch Operations and Performance

viewport.title: Viewport and Camera Control
viewport.content:
# Viewport and Camera Control

## Overview

Viewport control tools enable programmatic camera positioning, framing, and preset management. These tools are essential for automated scene photography, camera animation setup, and consistent camera positioning across multiple scenes.

## Key Concepts

### Camera Types

DAZ Studio cameras are `DzCamera` objects with standard node properties:

- **Position properties**: XTranslate, YTranslate, ZTranslate (in centimeters)
- **Rotation properties**: XRotate, YRotate, ZRotate (in degrees)
- **Scale properties**: XScale, YScale, ZScale (usually 1.0)

All cameras in the scene can be accessed via `Scene.getNumCameras()` / `Scene.getCamera(i)`.

### Active Viewport Camera

The **active viewport camera** determines what you see in the DAZ Studio 3D viewport. Changing the active camera updates the viewport view immediately:

```javascript
var viewportMgr = MainWindow.getViewportMgr();
var activeViewport = viewportMgr.getActiveViewport();
var viewport3D = activeViewport.get3DViewport();

// Get current camera
var currentCam = viewport3D.getCamera();

// Set new camera
viewport3D.setCamera(newCamera);
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_set_active_camera(camera_label)`

### Camera Coordinate System

Genesis figures face **+Z** (positive Z is in front):

- **+Z**: Front of figure
- **-Z**: Back of figure
- **+X**: Figure's right
- **-X**: Figure's left
- **+Y**: Up
- **-Y**: Down

When positioning cameras:
- Place at positive Z to see the face
- Place at negative Z for back view
- Positive Y values are above the figure

### Camera Aiming

The `.aimAt(DzVec3)` method automatically rotates a camera to point at a world-space position:

```javascript
var targetPos = node.getWSPos();
camera.aimAt(targetPos);
```

This is the correct way to point cameras. Do NOT attempt to set rotation properties manually or use "Point At" property.

**MCP Tool**: All camera positioning tools automatically aim the camera at the target.

## Camera Positioning Tools

### Set Active Camera

Switch which camera is active in the viewport:

```javascript
// MCP tool
daz_set_active_camera("Camera 1")

// Returns previous camera for easy switching back
{
  "success": true,
  "camera": "Camera 1",
  "previousCamera": "Perspective View"
}
```

**Use cases**:
- Switch between predefined camera angles
- Preview scene from multiple viewpoints
- Set up camera for render

### Orbit Camera Around Target

Position camera at specific angle around a target using spherical coordinates:

```javascript
// Position camera 45° to the right, 15° above, 200cm away
daz_orbit_camera_around(
    camera_label="Camera 1",
    target_label="Genesis 9",
    distance=200,
    angle_horizontal=45,
    angle_vertical=15
)
```

**Angle system**:
- **Horizontal** (yaw): 0°=front(+Z), 90°=right(+X), 180°=back(-Z), -90°=left(-X)
- **Vertical** (pitch): positive=above horizon, negative=below

**Common angles**:
```python
# Front 3/4 view
daz_orbit_camera_around("Camera 1", "Genesis 9", 200, 45, 15)

# Side view from left
daz_orbit_camera_around("Camera 1", "Genesis 9", 150, -90, 0)

# Bird's eye view
daz_orbit_camera_around("Camera 1", "Genesis 9", 300, 0, 60)

# Low angle (dramatic)
daz_orbit_camera_around("Camera 1", "Genesis 9", 180, 25, -20)

# Back view
daz_orbit_camera_around("Camera 1", "Genesis 9", 200, 180, 10)
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_orbit_camera_around(camera_label, target_label, distance, angle_horizontal, angle_vertical)`

### Frame Camera to Node

Automatically position camera to frame a node in view:

```javascript
// Auto-calculate distance based on object size
daz_frame_camera_to_node("Camera 1", "Genesis 9")

// Specific distance for close-up
daz_frame_camera_to_node("Camera 1", "head", distance=30)

// Frame a prop
daz_frame_camera_to_node("Camera 1", "Sword", distance=50)
```

**How it works**:
1. Calculates node's bounding box (3D extent)
2. Gets center point of bounding box
3. Calculates distance as 2.5x largest dimension (or uses provided distance)
4. Positions camera in front (+Z) at calculated distance
5. Aims camera at center

**Returns**:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "camera": "Camera 1",
  "node": "Genesis 9",
  "position": {"x": 0, "y": 100, "z": 450},
  "nodeCenter": {"x": 0, "y": 100, "z": 0},
  "nodeSize": {"x": 50, "y": 170, "z": 40}
}
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_frame_camera_to_node(camera_label, node_label, distance=None)`

## Camera Presets

### Save Camera Preset

Capture current camera position/rotation as reusable preset data:

```javascript
// Save camera state
preset = daz_save_camera_preset("Camera 1")

// Returns:
{
  "preset": {
    "label": "Camera 1",
    "transforms": {
      "XTranslate": 0,
      "YTranslate": 100,
      "ZTranslate": 300,
      "XRotate": -10,
      "YRotate": 0,
      "ZRotate": 0,
      "XScale": 1.0,
      "YScale": 1.0,
      "ZScale": 1.0
    }
  }
}
```

**Preset data** is a plain dictionary that can be:
- Saved to JSON files
- Stored in databases
- Shared between scenes
- Applied to any camera (not just the original)

**MCP Tool**: `daz_save_camera_preset(camera_label)`

### Load Camera Preset

Restore camera from saved preset data:

```python
# Load preset from file
import json
with open("my_camera_preset.json") as f:
    preset = json.load(f)

# Apply to camera
daz_load_camera_preset("Camera 1", preset["preset"])

# Apply same preset to multiple cameras
cameras = ["Camera 1", "Camera 2", "Camera 3"]
for cam in cameras:
    daz_load_camera_preset(cam, preset["preset"])
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_load_camera_preset(camera_label, preset)`

## Common Workflows

### Multi-Camera Setup

Set up multiple cameras for different angles:

```python
# Create cameras
daz_execute('var cam = new DzBasicCamera(); Scene.addNode(cam); cam.setLabel("Front");')
daz_execute('var cam = new DzBasicCamera(); Scene.addNode(cam); cam.setLabel("Side");')
daz_execute('var cam = new DzBasicCamera(); Scene.addNode(cam); cam.setLabel("Top");')

# Position cameras
daz_orbit_camera_around("Front", "Genesis 9", 200, 0, 15)
daz_orbit_camera_around("Side", "Genesis 9", 200, 90, 10)
daz_orbit_camera_around("Top", "Genesis 9", 300, 0, 75)

# Switch between views
daz_set_active_camera("Front")
# ... preview ...
daz_set_active_camera("Side")
# ... preview ...
```

### Consistent Framing Across Scenes

Save and reuse camera positions:

```python
# In scene 1: frame character and save position
daz_frame_camera_to_node("Camera 1", "Genesis 9")
preset = daz_save_camera_preset("Camera 1")

# Save to file
import json
with open("portrait_camera.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(preset, f)

# In scene 2: apply same camera position
with open("portrait_camera.json") as f:
    preset = json.load(f)
daz_load_camera_preset("Camera 1", preset["preset"])
```

### Automated Camera Tour

Create camera positions for animation or turntable:

```python
# Set up 8 cameras around character (360° turntable)
angles = [0, 45, 90, 135, 180, 225, 270, 315]
cameras = []

for i, angle in enumerate(angles):
    cam_name = f"Cam_{angle}"
    
    # Create camera
    daz_execute(f'''
        var cam = new DzBasicCamera();
        Scene.addNode(cam);
        cam.setLabel("{cam_name}");
    ''')
    
    # Position camera
    daz_orbit_camera_around(cam_name, "Genesis 9", 200, angle, 15)
    
    cameras.append(cam_name)

# Preview each angle
for cam in cameras:
    daz_set_active_camera(cam)
    # ... render or preview ...
```

### Dynamic Framing

Frame camera to different body parts:

```python
# Get hierarchy to find body parts
hierarchy = daz_get_node_hierarchy("Genesis 9", max_depth=2)

# Frame camera to face
daz_frame_camera_to_node("Camera 1", "head", distance=30)

# Frame camera to hands (for detail shot)
daz_frame_camera_to_node("Camera 1", "lHand", distance=20)

# Frame full body
daz_frame_camera_to_node("Camera 1", "Genesis 9", distance=400)
```

## Best Practices

### Camera Positioning

1. **Use orbit tool for character photography**: Spherical coordinates are natural for portraits and character shots

2. **Use frame tool for variable-size objects**: Auto-calculates appropriate distance based on object size

3. **Save presets for reusable angles**: Common angles (portrait, full body, over-shoulder) can be saved and reused

4. **Test multiple angles**: Set up several cameras and switch between them to find the best composition

### Distance Guidelines

**Character shots** (170cm tall figure):
- **Full body**: 350-450cm
- **Portrait (head/shoulders)**: 80-120cm
- **Face close-up**: 30-50cm
- **Detail (eyes, hands)**: 15-25cm

**Props and objects**: Use auto-framing (`distance=None`) initially, then adjust if needed.

### Angle Guidelines

**Flattering portrait angles**:
- Horizontal: 15-45° (slight angle, not straight on)
- Vertical: 5-15° (slightly above eye level)

**Dramatic angles**:
- Horizontal: 25-45°
- Vertical: -20 to -30° (low angle = powerful)

**Environmental shots**:
- Horizontal: 0-90° (varies by composition)
- Vertical: 30-60° (elevated view shows spatial relationships)

### Preset Management

1. **Name presets descriptively**: "portrait_front_3quarter", "full_body_side", "dramatic_low_angle"

2. **Organize by shot type**: Group presets by use case (portraits, full body, action shots, etc.)

3. **Version presets**: Save variations (tight/loose framing, different angles) for flexibility

4. **Share presets across projects**: Use consistent camera positions for visual continuity

## DazScript Camera API Reference

### Camera Creation

```javascript
// Create basic camera
var cam = new DzBasicCamera();
Scene.addNode(cam);
cam.setLabel("My Camera");
```

**NEVER** use `DzNewCameraAction` or other action classes - they pop modal dialogs and cause timeouts.

### Camera Properties

Standard node transform properties:
- `XTranslate`, `YTranslate`, `ZTranslate` (position in cm)
- `XRotate`, `YRotate`, `ZRotate` (rotation in degrees)
- `XScale`, `YScale`, `ZScale` (usually 1.0)

### Camera Positioning

```javascript
// Set position
cam.findProperty("XTranslate").setValue(x);
cam.findProperty("YTranslate").setValue(y);
cam.findProperty("ZTranslate").setValue(z);

// Aim at target
var targetPos = new DzVec3(x, y, z);
cam.aimAt(targetPos);
```

### Viewport Control

```javascript
// Get viewport manager
var viewportMgr = MainWindow.getViewportMgr();
var activeViewport = viewportMgr.getActiveViewport();
var viewport3D = activeViewport.get3DViewport();

// Get/set camera
var currentCam = viewport3D.getCamera();
viewport3D.setCamera(newCamera);
```

### Bounding Box (for framing)

```javascript
// Get node bounding box
var bbox = node.getSceneBoundingBox();
var center = bbox.getCenter();    // DzVec3
var size = bbox.getSize();        // DzVec3 (width, height, depth)

// Calculate max dimension
var maxDim = Math.max(size.x, Math.max(size.y, size.z));

// Position camera at calculated distance
var distance = maxDim * 2.5;
```

## Troubleshooting

**Camera doesn't move**: Ensure you're setting properties correctly and camera exists. Use `daz_scene_info()` to list cameras.

**Viewport doesn't update**: The viewport should update immediately when setting active camera. If not, try switching to another camera and back.

**Object not framed correctly**: The auto-framing uses bounding box center. For figures, this may be lower than desired (at waist level). Use orbit tool with specific angles instead, or frame to "head" bone for face shots.

**Preset doesn't restore correctly**: Ensure preset dictionary structure is correct (must have "transforms" key). Check that all property names are valid.

**Camera too close/far**: Adjust distance parameter. Remember distances are in centimeters (100cm = 1m).

animation.title: Animation System and Keyframing
animation.content:
# Animation System and Keyframing

## Overview

DAZ Studio's animation system uses keyframe-based animation. You set keyframes (specific values at specific frames) on numeric properties, and DAZ Studio interpolates between keyframes to create smooth motion. These tools enable programmatic animation creation, keyframe management, and timeline control.

## Key Concepts

### Keyframes

A **keyframe** is a property value at a specific frame number. DAZ Studio automatically interpolates values between keyframes:

```javascript
// Frame 0: XTranslate = 0
// Frame 30: XTranslate = 100
// Frame 15 (automatic): XTranslate = 50 (interpolated)
```

### Timeline

The **timeline** defines:
- **Current frame**: Where you are in the animation
- **Frame range**: Start and end frames (inclusive)
- **FPS**: Frames per second (typically 30)

### Property Animation

Any numeric property can be animated:
- **Transforms**: XTranslate, YTranslate, ZTranslate, XRotate, YRotate, ZRotate
- **Morphs**: Facial expressions, body shapes, corrective morphs
- **Light properties**: Flux (intensity), Shadow Softness, color
- **Camera properties**: Focal length, DOF

## Creating Animations

### Basic Animation Workflow

1. **Set frame range** (define animation length)
2. **Set keyframes** on properties at different frames
3. **Preview** by setting current frame
4. **Render** each frame to create image sequence

### Simple Translation Animation

Move a character 100cm to the right over 1 second (30 frames at 30fps):

```python
# Set animation length
daz_set_frame_range(0, 29)  # 30 frames = 1 second

# Set start keyframe (frame 0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=0, value=0)

# Set end keyframe (frame 29)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=29, value=100)

# DAZ Studio interpolates frames 1-28 automatically
```

**MCP Tools**: `daz_set_frame_range()`, `daz_set_keyframe()`

### Rotation Animation

Rotate character 360° over 4 seconds (120 frames):

```python
# Set animation length (4 seconds at 30fps)
daz_set_frame_range(0, 119)

# Full rotation
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "YRotate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "YRotate", frame=119, value=360)
```

### Morph Animation

Animate facial expression (fade in smile):

```python
# 2-second animation
daz_set_frame_range(0, 59)

# Neutral at start
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "PHMSmile", frame=0, value=0)

# Full smile at end
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "PHMSmile", frame=59, value=0.8)
```

### Multi-Property Animation

Animate multiple properties together (walk forward while waving):

```python
daz_set_frame_range(0, 90)  # 3 seconds

# Walk forward
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "ZTranslate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "ZTranslate", frame=90, value=150)

# Wave arm (up and down)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9/rShldrBend", "ZRotate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9/rShldrBend", "ZRotate", frame=30, value=45)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9/rShldrBend", "ZRotate", frame=60, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9/rShldrBend", "ZRotate", frame=90, value=45)
```

## Keyframe Management

### Inspecting Keyframes

Get all keyframes for a property:

```python
result = daz_get_keyframes("Genesis 9", "XTranslate")
print(f"Found {result['count']} keyframes:")
for kf in result['keyframes']:
    print(f"  Frame {kf['frame']}: {kf['value']}")

# Output:
# Found 2 keyframes:
#   Frame 0: 0.0
#   Frame 29: 100.0
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_get_keyframes(node_label, property_name)`

### Removing Keyframes

Remove single keyframe:

```python
# Remove keyframe at frame 15
daz_remove_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=15)
```

Clear all keyframes from property:

```python
# Remove all keyframes
result = daz_clear_animation("Genesis 9", "XTranslate")
print(f"Removed {result['removed']} keyframes")
```

**MCP Tools**: `daz_remove_keyframe()`, `daz_clear_animation()`

### Copying Keyframes

Copy animation from one property to another:

```python
# Get keyframes from source
keyframes = daz_get_keyframes("Genesis 9", "XTranslate")

# Apply to target
for kf in keyframes['keyframes']:
    daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 8", "XTranslate", kf['frame'], kf['value'])
```

### Offsetting Animation

Shift animation in time:

```python
# Get original keyframes
keyframes = daz_get_keyframes("Genesis 9", "XTranslate")

# Clear original
daz_clear_animation("Genesis 9", "XTranslate")

# Re-add with 30-frame offset
for kf in keyframes['keyframes']:
    daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", kf['frame'] + 30, kf['value'])
```

## Timeline Control

### Get Timeline Info

```python
info = daz_get_animation_info()
print(f"Current frame: {info['currentFrame']}")
print(f"Range: {info['startFrame']}-{info['endFrame']}")
print(f"FPS: {info['fps']}")
print(f"Duration: {info['durationSeconds']} seconds")
print(f"Total frames: {info['totalFrames']}")

# Output:
# Current frame: 0
# Range: 0-119
# FPS: 30.0
# Duration: 4.0 seconds
# Total frames: 120
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_get_animation_info()`

### Set Current Frame

Move timeline to specific frame:

```python
# Jump to frame 30
daz_set_frame(30)

# Jump to middle of animation
info = daz_get_animation_info()
mid_frame = (info['startFrame'] + info['endFrame']) // 2
daz_set_frame(mid_frame)
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_set_frame(frame)`

### Set Frame Range

Define animation length:

```python
# 1 second (30 frames at 30fps)
daz_set_frame_range(0, 29)

# 10 seconds (300 frames at 30fps)
daz_set_frame_range(0, 299)

# Custom start frame
daz_set_frame_range(10, 100)
```

**Frame count calculation**:
- Total frames = end - start + 1 (range is inclusive)
- Duration in seconds = total frames / fps
- Example: frames 0-29 = 30 frames = 1 second at 30fps

**MCP Tool**: `daz_set_frame_range(start_frame, end_frame)`

## Rendering Animations

### Render All Frames

Export animation as image sequence:

```python
import os

# Set up output directory
os.makedirs("animation_output", exist_ok=True)

# Get animation info
info = daz_get_animation_info()

# Render each frame
for frame in range(info['startFrame'], info['endFrame'] + 1):
    daz_set_frame(frame)
    output_path = f"animation_output/frame_{frame:04d}.png"
    daz_render(output_path=output_path)
    print(f"Rendered frame {frame}/{info['endFrame']}")
```

### Render Specific Frames

Render only keyframes:

```python
# Get keyframes
keyframes = daz_get_keyframes("Genesis 9", "XTranslate")

# Render each keyframe
for kf in keyframes['keyframes']:
    daz_set_frame(kf['frame'])
    daz_render(output_path=f"keyframe_{kf['frame']:04d}.png")
```

Render every Nth frame (preview quality):

```python
info = daz_get_animation_info()

# Render every 5th frame
for frame in range(info['startFrame'], info['endFrame'] + 1, 5):
    daz_set_frame(frame)
    daz_render(output_path=f"preview_{frame:04d}.png")
```

## Common Animation Patterns

### Looping Animation

Create seamless loop by matching start and end values:

```python
# Rotation loop (360° returns to start)
daz_set_frame_range(0, 119)
daz_set_keyframe("Prop", "YRotate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Prop", "YRotate", frame=119, value=360)

# Morph loop (return to neutral)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "PHMSmile", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "PHMSmile", frame=60, value=1.0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "PHMSmile", frame=119, value=0)
```

### Easing with Extra Keyframes

Add keyframes for custom timing (ease in/out):

```python
# Linear: just start and end
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=60, value=100)

# Ease out: slow down at end
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=40, value=80)  # 80% by frame 40
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=60, value=100)  # last 20% over 20 frames

# Ease in: slow start
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=20, value=20)  # slow start
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "XTranslate", frame=60, value=100)
```

### Camera Animation

Animate camera for cinematic shots:

```python
daz_set_frame_range(0, 150)  # 5 seconds

# Camera moves closer
daz_set_keyframe("Camera 1", "ZTranslate", frame=0, value=300)
daz_set_keyframe("Camera 1", "ZTranslate", frame=150, value=150)

# Camera tilts up
daz_set_keyframe("Camera 1", "XRotate", frame=0, value=-5)
daz_set_keyframe("Camera 1", "XRotate", frame=150, value=5)
```

### Turntable Animation

360° product-style rotation:

```python
# Set up turntable (4 seconds, 360°)
daz_set_frame_range(0, 119)

# Rotate figure
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "YRotate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "YRotate", frame=119, value=360)

# Optional: rotate camera instead of figure
daz_set_keyframe("Camera 1", "YRotate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Camera 1", "YRotate", frame=119, value=-360)  # opposite direction
```

### Multi-Character Animation

Animate multiple characters with offset timing:

```python
daz_set_frame_range(0, 90)

# Character 1 starts at frame 0
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "ZTranslate", frame=0, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "ZTranslate", frame=60, value=100)

# Character 2 starts at frame 30 (delayed)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 8", "ZTranslate", frame=30, value=0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 8", "ZTranslate", frame=90, value=100)
```

## DazScript Animation API Reference

### Keyframe Management

```javascript
// Set keyframe
prop.setKeyFrame(frame, value);

// Get number of keyframes
var numKeys = prop.getNumKeys();

// Get keyframe frame/value
var frame = prop.getKeyFrame(index);
var value = prop.getKeyValue(index);

// Delete keyframe
prop.deleteKey(index);
```

### Timeline Control

```javascript
// Get/set current frame
var currentFrame = Scene.getFrame();
Scene.setFrame(30);

// Get frame range
var anim = Scene.getAnimRange();
var startFrame = anim.getStart();
var endFrame = anim.getEnd();

// Set frame range
anim.setStart(0);
anim.setEnd(119);

// Get FPS
var fps = Scene.getFPS();
```

## Best Practices

### Frame Numbering

- Use 0-based frame numbering (0, 1, 2, ...)
- Frame range is **inclusive** (frames 0-29 = 30 frames)
- Typical FPS is 30 in DAZ Studio

### Duration Calculation

```python
# Duration in seconds
duration = (end_frame - start_frame + 1) / fps

# Example: 30 frames at 30fps = 1 second
# (29 - 0 + 1) / 30 = 30 / 30 = 1.0
```

### Performance

- Minimize keyframes (DAZ interpolates smoothly)
- Use 2-3 keyframes per motion, not one per frame
- Clear unused animations to reduce scene complexity

### Animation Workflow

1. **Plan**: Decide animation length and key poses
2. **Set range**: Define frame range first
3. **Block**: Set keyframes at key poses
4. **Refine**: Add intermediate keyframes for timing
5. **Preview**: Use daz_set_frame() to check motion
6. **Render**: Export all frames as image sequence

### Naming Rendered Frames

Use zero-padded frame numbers for proper sorting:

```python
# Good: sorts correctly (frame_0001.png, frame_0002.png, ...)
output_path = f"frame_{frame:04d}.png"

# Bad: sorts incorrectly (frame_1.png, frame_10.png, frame_2.png, ...)
output_path = f"frame_{frame}.png"
```

### Converting to Video

After rendering image sequence, use ffmpeg to create video:

```bash
# Convert PNG sequence to MP4 (30fps)
ffmpeg -framerate 30 -i frame_%04d.png -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p output.mp4
```

## Troubleshooting

**Animation not smooth**: Add more keyframes at key poses. DAZ interpolates linearly between keyframes.

**Wrong frame count**: Remember frame range is inclusive. Frames 0-29 = 30 frames, not 29.

**Render starts at wrong frame**: Use daz_set_frame(start_frame) before rendering.

**Animation resets**: Check that keyframes are actually set. Use daz_get_keyframes() to verify.

**Interpolation jumps**: If property wraps (like rotation at 360°), avoid crossing the wrap boundary. Instead of 350°→10°, use 350°→370°.

rendering.title: Advanced Rendering Control
rendering.content:
# Advanced Rendering Control

## Overview

Advanced rendering tools provide programmatic control over render operations, enabling batch rendering from multiple cameras, animation export, and render configuration management. These tools complement the basic `daz_render()` tool with multi-camera and automation capabilities.

## Key Concepts

### Render Manager

The **render manager** (`App.getRenderMgr()`) controls all rendering operations:

```javascript
var renderMgr = App.getRenderMgr();
var opts = renderMgr.getRenderOptions();

// Configure render
opts.renderImgToId = 0;  // 0 = render to file, 1 = render to viewport
opts.renderImgFilename = "/path/to/output.png";

// Execute render
renderMgr.doRender();
```

### Render Options

Key render options accessible via `getRenderOptions()`:

- **`renderImgToId`**: 0 = file, 1 = viewport
- **`renderImgFilename`**: Output file path (when renderImgToId = 0)
- **`camera`**: DzCamera object to render from (null = use viewport camera)
- **`aspect`**: Aspect ratio
- **`aspectWidth`**, **`aspectHeight`**: Aspect ratio components

**Note**: Pixel dimensions (`setImageSize()`) are **not** reliably available in this DazScript environment. Set render dimensions in DAZ Studio's render settings UI before using these tools.

### Render vs Viewport Camera

- **Viewport camera**: What you see in the 3D viewport (controlled by `daz_set_active_camera()`)
- **Render camera**: Camera used for rendering (can be different from viewport camera)

The `daz_render_with_camera()` tool sets render camera without changing viewport, allowing non-disruptive multi-camera renders.

## Multi-Camera Rendering

### Render from Specific Camera

Render from a camera without changing the viewport:

```python
# Render from specific camera
daz_render_with_camera("Camera 1", output_path="/renders/cam1.png")

# Viewport camera unchanged - user's view is not disrupted
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_render_with_camera(camera_label, output_path)`

**Use cases**:
- Multi-camera batch renders
- Testing camera angles without disrupting viewport
- Automated rendering workflows

### Batch Render Multiple Cameras

Render from multiple cameras in one operation:

```python
# Render from 4 cameras
daz_batch_render_cameras(
    cameras=["Front", "Side", "Top", "Perspective"],
    output_dir="/path/to/renders",
    base_filename="character"
)

# Generates:
# /path/to/renders/character_Front.png
# /path/to/renders/character_Side.png
# /path/to/renders/character_Top.png
# /path/to/renders/character_Perspective.png
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_batch_render_cameras(cameras, output_dir, base_filename)`

**Filename convention**: Camera names have non-alphanumeric characters replaced with underscores.

**Common patterns**:

*Turntable renders (8 angles)*:
```python
# Set up cameras around character
angles = [0, 45, 90, 135, 180, 225, 270, 315]
cameras = []
for angle in angles:
    cam_name = f"Cam_{angle}"
    # Create and position camera
    daz_execute(f'var c = new DzBasicCamera(); Scene.addNode(c); c.setLabel("{cam_name}");')
    daz_orbit_camera_around(cam_name, "Genesis 9", 200, angle, 15)
    cameras.append(cam_name)

# Render all angles
daz_batch_render_cameras(cameras, "/renders/turntable", "angle")
```

*Render all cameras in scene*:
```python
scene_info = daz_scene_info()
all_cameras = [cam['label'] for cam in scene_info['cameras']]
daz_batch_render_cameras(all_cameras, "/renders/all_views")
```

## Animation Rendering

### Render Animation as Image Sequence

Export animation by rendering each frame:

```python
# Render entire animation (uses animation range)
daz_render_animation(output_dir="/path/to/animation")

# Generates:
# frame_0000.png, frame_0001.png, ..., frame_0119.png
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_render_animation(output_dir, start_frame, end_frame, filename_pattern, camera)`

**Frame numbering**: Automatically zero-padded to 4 digits (0000-9999) for proper sorting.

### Render Specific Frame Range

Render subset of animation:

```python
# Render frames 30-60 only
daz_render_animation(
    output_dir="/path/to/clip",
    start_frame=30,
    end_frame=60,
    filename_pattern="clip"
)
```

### Render Animation from Specific Camera

```python
# Render animation from Camera 1
daz_render_animation(
    output_dir="/path/to/animation",
    camera="Camera 1"
)
```

### Comparison: Manual vs Automated Rendering

**Manual approach (35 tools calls for 120 frames)**:
```python
info = daz_get_animation_info()
for frame in range(info['startFrame'], info['endFrame'] + 1):
    daz_set_frame(frame)
    daz_render(output_path=f"animation/frame_{frame:04d}.png")
```

**Automated approach (1 tool call)**:
```python
daz_render_animation(output_dir="animation")
```

Benefits of automated rendering:
- Single tool call (reduces HTTP overhead)
- Automatic frame range detection
- Zero-padded filenames
- Timeline restoration
- Cleaner code

### Preview Rendering

Render subset of frames for quick preview:

```python
# Render every 5th frame
info = daz_get_animation_info()
for frame in range(info['startFrame'], info['endFrame'] + 1, 5):
    daz_render_animation(
        output_dir="/preview",
        start_frame=frame,
        end_frame=frame
    )
```

## Render Settings Management

### Query Render Settings

Check current render configuration:

```python
settings = daz_get_render_settings()
print(f"Render to file: {settings['renderToFile']}")
print(f"Output path: {settings['outputPath']}")
print(f"Render camera: {settings['currentCamera']}")
print(f"Aspect: {settings['aspectWidth']}x{settings['aspectHeight']}")
```

**MCP Tool**: `daz_get_render_settings()`

**Use cases**:
- Verify render configuration before batch operations
- Debug render issues
- Check which camera will be used

### Validate Before Batch Render

```python
# Check settings before long batch render
settings = daz_get_render_settings()

if not settings['renderToFile']:
    print("ERROR: Render is configured for viewport, not file output")
    # Configure in DAZ Studio UI or abort

if settings['currentCamera'] is None:
    print("Using viewport camera for renders")
```

## Common Workflows

### Multi-Angle Product Shots

Set up and render multiple product angles:

```python
# 1. Position object
daz_set_property("Product", "YRotate", 0)

# 2. Create cameras at standard angles
angles = {
    "front": (0, 10),
    "front_left": (-45, 15),
    "front_right": (45, 15),
    "left": (-90, 10),
    "right": (90, 10),
    "top": (0, 75)
}

for name, (h_angle, v_angle) in angles.items():
    cam_name = f"Product_{name}"
    daz_execute(f'var c = new DzBasicCamera(); Scene.addNode(c); c.setLabel("{cam_name}");')
    daz_orbit_camera_around(cam_name, "Product", 250, h_angle, v_angle)

# 3. Batch render all angles
camera_list = [f"Product_{name}" for name in angles.keys()]
daz_batch_render_cameras(camera_list, "/renders/product", "product")
```

### Character Turntable Animation

Create and render 360° turntable:

```python
# 1. Set up animation (4 seconds, 360°)
daz_set_frame_range(0, 119)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "YRotate", 0, 0)
daz_set_keyframe("Genesis 9", "YRotate", 119, 360)

# 2. Position camera
daz_orbit_camera_around("Camera 1", "Genesis 9", 200, 45, 15)

# 3. Render animation
daz_render_animation("/renders/turntable", camera="Camera 1")

# 4. Convert to video (outside Python)
# ffmpeg -framerate 30 -i turntable/frame_%04d.png -c:v libx264 turntable.mp4
```

### Multi-Camera Animation

Render same animation from multiple camera angles:

```python
# 1. Set up animation
daz_set_frame_range(0, 90)
# ... create animation ...

# 2. Set up cameras
cameras = ["Close_Up", "Medium_Shot", "Wide_Shot"]
for cam in cameras:
    daz_execute(f'var c = new DzBasicCamera(); Scene.addNode(c); c.setLabel("{cam}");')

# Position cameras
daz_orbit_camera_around("Close_Up", "Genesis 9", 120, 25, 10)
daz_orbit_camera_around("Medium_Shot", "Genesis 9", 200, 35, 12)
daz_orbit_camera_around("Wide_Shot", "Genesis 9", 400, 45, 15)

# 3. Render animation from each camera
for cam in cameras:
    output_dir = f"/renders/{cam}"
    daz_render_animation(output_dir, camera=cam)
```

### Test Renders from Multiple Angles

Quick test renders before final render:

```python
# Set up test cameras
test_angles = [0, 45, 90, 180]
for angle in test_angles:
    cam_name = f"Test_{angle}"
    daz_execute(f'var c = new DzBasicCamera(); Scene.addNode(c); c.setLabel("{cam_name}");')
    daz_orbit_camera_around(cam_name, "Genesis 9", 200, angle, 15)

# Render low-res tests
test_cameras = [f"Test_{a}" for a in test_angles]
daz_batch_render_cameras(test_cameras, "/test_renders", "test")

# Review renders, pick best angle, then do final high-res render
daz_render_with_camera("Test_45", "/final/final_render.png")
```

## Video Conversion

After rendering animation as image sequence, convert to video using **ffmpeg**:

### Basic Conversion

```bash
# Convert PNG sequence to MP4 (30fps, standard quality)
ffmpeg -framerate 30 -i frame_%04d.png -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p output.mp4
```

### High Quality Conversion

```bash
# High quality with slower encoding
ffmpeg -framerate 30 -i frame_%04d.png -c:v libx264 -preset slow -crf 18 -pix_fmt yuv420p output.mp4
```

### Different Framerates

```bash
# 60fps (smooth)
ffmpeg -framerate 60 -i frame_%04d.png -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p output_60fps.mp4

# 24fps (cinematic)
ffmpeg -framerate 24 -i frame_%04d.png -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p output_24fps.mp4
```

### Loop Video

```bash
# Create looping video (10 loops)
ffmpeg -stream_loop 10 -framerate 30 -i frame_%04d.png -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p looping.mp4
```

### GIF Conversion

```bash
# Convert to animated GIF (useful for web previews)
ffmpeg -framerate 30 -i frame_%04d.png -vf "scale=640:-1" output.gif

# Optimized GIF with palette
ffmpeg -framerate 30 -i frame_%04d.png -vf "fps=15,scale=320:-1:flags=lanczos,split[s0][s1];[s0]palettegen[p];[s1][p]paletteuse" output_optimized.gif
```

## Best Practices

### File Organization

Organize renders by purpose:

```
/renders/
  /test/           # Low-res test renders
  /camera_test/    # Multi-angle camera tests
  /final/          # Final high-res renders
  /animation/      # Animation frame sequences
    /cam1/
    /cam2/
  /turntable/      # Product turntables
```

### Filename Conventions

- Use descriptive base names: `character_portrait_`, not `render_`
- Zero-pad frame numbers: `frame_0001.png`, not `frame_1.png`
- Include camera/angle in multi-camera renders: `product_front.png`, `product_side.png`
- Use underscores, not spaces: `my_render.png`, not `my render.png`

### Render Verification

Check settings before long renders:

```python
# Verify animation settings
info = daz_get_animation_info()
print(f"Will render {info['totalFrames']} frames ({info['durationSeconds']}s)")

# Verify render settings
settings = daz_get_render_settings()
if settings['currentCamera']:
    print(f"Using camera: {settings['currentCamera']}")

# Estimate time (if 1 frame takes 2 minutes)
frames = info['totalFrames']
print(f"Estimated time: {frames * 2} minutes ({frames * 2 / 60:.1f} hours)")

# Confirm before proceeding
proceed = input("Continue? (y/n): ")
if proceed.lower() == 'y':
    daz_render_animation("/renders/animation")
```

### Progressive Rendering Strategy

1. **Test render**: Single frame at low resolution
2. **Camera tests**: Multiple angles, low resolution
3. **Preview animation**: Every 5th or 10th frame
4. **Final render**: Full resolution, all frames/cameras

### Batch Processing

For large batch renders, consider:
- Render overnight or during off-hours
- Monitor first few frames for issues before committing to full batch
- Save intermediate results (don't rely on one massive operation)
- Use `daz_render_animation()` instead of manual frame loops (more reliable)

## Performance Considerations

### Rendering Speed

Rendering is typically the slowest operation in a DAZ Studio workflow:

- **Single frame**: 30 seconds to 5+ minutes (depends on scene complexity, quality settings)
- **120-frame animation**: 1-10 hours
- **Multi-camera batch** (8 cameras): 4-40 minutes

### Optimization Tips

1. **Lower quality for tests**: Use DAZ Studio's draft/preview quality settings for test renders
2. **Reduce samples**: Lower iteration count for faster (but noisier) renders
3. **Render subsets**: Test with small frame ranges before full animation
4. **Parallel rendering**: Render different cameras/frame ranges on different machines if available

### HTTP Timeout Considerations

- Rendering operations can take **minutes** per frame
- Default timeout is 30 seconds (`DAZ_TIMEOUT` env var)
- For rendering, increase timeout: `DAZ_TIMEOUT=300` (5 minutes) or higher
- Batch operations (`daz_render_animation`, `daz_batch_render_cameras`) may take **hours**

## Troubleshooting

**Render produces black image**: Check that scene has lights. Use `daz_scene_info()` to list lights. Verify camera is positioned correctly and looking at scene.

**"Camera not found" error**: Verify camera name with `daz_scene_info()`. Camera labels are case-sensitive.

**Timeout during animation render**: Increase `DAZ_TIMEOUT` environment variable. Animation rendering can take hours.

**Wrong aspect ratio**: Aspect ratio is set in DAZ Studio render settings UI. Cannot be reliably set via DazScript.

**Renders from wrong camera**: Use `daz_get_render_settings()` to check which camera will be used. Set explicitly with `daz_render_with_camera()` or `camera` parameter.

**File not created**: Check that `outputPath` is absolute path with valid directory. Verify write permissions. Check DAZ Studio log for errors.

## DazScript Render API Reference

### Render Manager

```javascript
var renderMgr = App.getRenderMgr();

// Get render options
var opts = renderMgr.getRenderOptions();

// Execute render
renderMgr.doRender();
```

### Render Options

```javascript
// Render to file
opts.renderImgToId = 0;
opts.renderImgFilename = "/path/to/output.png";

// Render to viewport
opts.renderImgToId = 1;

// Set render camera
opts.camera = cameraNode;  // DzCamera object

// Get aspect ratio
var aspect = opts.aspect;
var width = opts.aspectWidth;
var height = opts.aspectHeight;
```

### Important Notes

- **`setImageSize()` is not available** in this DazScript environment
- Set pixel dimensions in DAZ Studio UI before using render tools
- Render settings persist between renders
- Camera can be null (uses active viewport camera)
